steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes describe two neon sculptures: one spells out a concrete phrase, the other is an abstract form. The question asks for a sentence that emphasizes this contrast in how each sculpture uses neon.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — While the sculptures share a common material, one spells out a phrase while the other is decidedly more abstract.: directly contrasts the two sculptures' forms — phrase vs. abstraction — which is the key distinction in the notes. ✓
  • Choice B — Molded into an evocative phrase in *Use the Future* (1992), an abstract form in *Ginger* (2023), and an advertising tool in decades past, neon lighting has a number of different applications.: treats the form difference as secondary to a wider point about neon in general, losing the specific contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — Italian artist Maurizio Nannucci's and Japanese artist Rikako Kawauchi's sculptures have employed neon signs in different ways, demonstrating the versatility of the popular material.: states vaguely that the sculptures use neon signs in different ways but does not specify the key contrast between phrase and abstract form. ✗
  • Choice D — Nannucci's sculpture was made in 1992 and Kawauchi's in 2023, both well after the advertising heyday of neon signs had passed.: redirects to dates and neon's advertising history rather than the formal contrast the notes establish. ✗
That matches Choice A — While the sculptures share a common material, one spells out a phrase while the other is decidedly more abstract..
